data.title: Rupee weakens to record low on US-India trade limbo, Fed rate outlook
data.description:
The Indian rupee weakened to a lifetime low against the dollar on Friday, dragged down by subdued risk appetite on fading expectations of a Federal Reserve rate cut and uncertainty over the U.S.–India trade impasse.

The rupee touched 88.83, slipping past its previous all-time low of 88.80 hit in late September and again earlier this month. It was down 0.1% on the day.

On the interbank order-matching system , losses were even sharper, with the currency last quoted at 89.34.

Pressure on the rupee has persisted since steep U.S. tariffs on Indian exports came into effect in late August. The currency is now among the weakest major Asian performers this year as foreign investors have withdrawn $16.5 billion from Indian equities so far.

On Friday, traders said that the central bank, which had actively defended the 88.80 level in recent sessions, appeared to have scaled back its intervention.

This compounded the pressure on the rupee, which is already contending with hedging interest from importers and muted activity from exporters.

There was a sudden spike in volumes once 88.80 broke, a trader at a private sector bank said.

data.title: Europe Must Look Inward to Drive Growth, ECB's Lagarde Says — Update
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

Europe must break down its internal barriers to move away from a growth model that is driven by exports, European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said.

In a speech to bankers Friday, Lagarde also highlighted Europe's vulnerability to the "weaponization" of key raw materials and technologies.

"Europe's vulnerabilities stem from having a growth model geared towards a world that is gradually disappearing," she said.

European policymakers have faced a number of unpleasant surprises over recent years. In 2022, Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, starting the largest and most sustained military conflict in Europe since World War II.

To the surprise of many economists, Europe avoided a recession as energy and food prices surged. But having weathered that storm, this year has seen the new U.S. administration put a longstanding military alliance in question and erect high barriers to trans-Atlantic trade.

"Exports have become a far less reliable engine of growth, reflecting the changing global landscape," Lagarde said. "Exports are projected to subtract from growth over the next two years."

Instead of relying on fading external demand, Lagarde said the European Union must quickly lower its internal barriers, which prevent businesses from offering goods and services in other countries without costly efforts to comply with local regulations.

"ECB analysis finds that internal barriers in services and goods markets are equivalent to tariffs of around 100% and 65%, respectively," Lagarde said.

The central banker said that in order to move with speed, the EU would have to make more decisions by majority voting and drop the practice of giving individual members a veto over significant changes.

"Reforms such as harmonizing VAT rules or establishing a common consolidated corporate tax base remain stuck because of national vetoes, leaving firms to navigate a maze of fragmented tax regimes," she said.

Lagarde also advocated for the creation of what are known as "28th regimes", or legal frameworks that apply across the EU which businesses could choose over national laws.

"A European company law regime would provide firms with a simpler path to operate across borders, cutting through the complexity of 27 different national systems," she said.

Both proposals face opposition from E.U. members that want to preserve national control over taxation and the activities of businesses.

However, Lagarde said the removal of national barriers is essential if Europe's economy is to move from being "merely resilient to being genuinely strong."

"If we make our Single Market truly single, Europe's growth will no longer depend on the decisions of others, but on our own choices," she said.

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

Daily performance is calculated by reference to the previous day NAV/share and VNAS (Vietnam All Share Index). Calendar month-to-date performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 31 Oct 2025. Calendar year-to-date performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS at the previous calendar year end (31 Dec 2024). 1-year performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 21 Nov 2024. 3-year CAGR (compound annual growth rate) is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 21 Nov 2022. 5-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 21 Nov 2020. 10-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 21 Nov 2015. 15-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 21 Nov 2010. Since inception CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share as of 30 Jun 2006. VNAS is sourced from Bloomberg, which uses back-tested positions for VNAS for before 27 Jan 2014 as released by HoSE (Ho Chi Minh City Stock Exchange).

The Estimated Daily NAV stated in this announcement is based on estimated valuations and accordingly the Company accepts no responsibility for the accuracy of the Estimated Daily NAV figure given, and neither is any responsibility implied. The actual month end NAV in any month may vary from the Estimated Daily NAV provided in the relevant month. Accordingly, no reliance should be placed on the Estimated Daily NAV, and it has been provided for indicative purposes only.

More information on the Company is available at www.vietnamholding.com

data.title: Corn Hits 4-week Low
data.description:
Corn decreased to 425.50 USd/BU, the lowest since October 2025.

Over the past 4 weeks, Corn gained 0.66%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 0.07%.

data.title: Technimont attends ground-breaking ceremony of Tengiz Gas separation Complex in Kazakhstan
data.description:
In a two-day event of work and activities, Tecnimont (MAIRE) held a ground-breaking ceremony at the Tengiz Gas Separation Complex (GSC) at the presence of local authorities and companies’ representatives working on the project development, as per Hydrocarbonprocessing.

The Tengiz Gas Separation Complex (GSC) includes full-scope engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning works, with Tecnimont mainly responsible for engineering, procurement and commissioning. The facility is designed to recover at least 98% of ethane from dry gas, and its output will feed the second phase of the Silleno petrochemical complex, focused on polyethylene production—another major project being executed by Tecnimont.

The Tengiz and Silleno projects mark a significant milestone for Kazakhstan, as it involves technologies and scale never implemented in the country and are seen as pioneering efforts that will strengthen the national petrochemical sector.

As a result, KMG PetroChem and Tecnimont, in collaboration with PetroCouncil (the Kazakhstan Oil & Gas Association), have launched a new “Local Value Shared Growth Forum” in Atyrau, Kazakhstan. The event addressed to the local supply chain follows the forum held last September for the Silleno project. This edition saw the participation of 400 potential local suppliers, local authorities and representatives of the organizing entities and the consortium partner.

The program featured targeted workshops, seminars, and networking sessions, equipping participants with essential tools and knowledge to thrive in their respective sectors. These efforts contribute to the broader development of the Atyrau community.

Alessandro Bernini, CEO of MAIRE commented: “This new edition of the “Local Value Shared Growth Forum” highlights the significance of ICV programs for both partners and the industrial sector. The collaboration with PetroCouncil, recently joined by Tecnimont KZ, illustrates the benefits of integrating with the local market and promoting economic development”.

mrchub.com

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • NONE

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • NONE

(c)        Attachments

  • Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?

  • NO

data.title: Siemens Energy falls 7% after Thursday's record high
data.description:
** Shares of Germany's Siemens Energy tumbled around 7% after hitting a record high on Thursday after its results

** A trader points to no more positive news being expected after the company's capital markets day and "AI turn"

** RBC, meanwhile, upgrades its target price to 136 euros from 130 euros per share, taking new mid-term forecast and outlook beyond into account

** Shares on track for their worst day since April

** Up to the previous session's close, shares were up 122.5%​ YTD

data.title: Babcock International Firing on All Cylinders — Market Talk
data.description: Babcock International's first-half results show that the U.K. defense and nuclear equipment manufacturer is well and firing on all cylinders, says Mark Crouch, market analyst for eToro. A 19% jump in first-half operating profit and a 25% dividend increase would be eye-catching in any sector, but especially in a business better known for long-cycle contracts and cautious guidance, he says. Crouch describes Babcock as one of the most consistent outperformers of the London market. The group's latest results suggest Babcock is no longer relying on geopolitical crisis alone; they are also due to discipline, he adds. Shares are down 1.2% at 11.16 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Brenntag acquires Texas production chemicals provider
data.description:
Brenntag SE has announced the acquisition of Chem Tech Services, Inc. (Levelland, Texas), a production chemicals provider for the energy sector in the Permian Basin, as per Chemweek.

Chem Tech Services will be integrated into Coastal Chemical, Brenntag’s platform for upstream, midstream and downstream segments for the oil and gas industry, the company said. No financial details were disclosed.Chem Tech Services had sales of over $80 million in the past 12 months. The company provides oilfield chemical solutions in Texas and New Mexico.“Chem Tech’s expertise and network are a welcome addition to Brenntag’s energy services business in North America, adding capabilities to our resilient market offerings in the energy sector and strengthening our market presence in a region that is critical to global energy supply,” Scoot Leibowitz, president Brenntag Essentials North America, said.This is Brenntag’s fifth acquisition this year, and second in the US. In August, Brenntag acquired TPE Midstream LLC, a temporary pipeline integrity solutions company based out of Oklahoma.Signing and closing of the deal took place simultaneously.

mrchub.com

data.title: Russian Ruble Hits 30-month High
data.description:
The Russian Ruble touched 76.55 against the USD, the highest since May 2023.

Over the past 4 weeks, US Dollar Russian Ruble lost 3.42%, and in the last 12 months, it decreased 24.57%.

NVRI: $3.04B Clean Earth sale and spin-off to unlock value, with cash and shares for shareholders
data.description:
Clean Earth will be sold to Veolia for $3.04 billion, with shareholders receiving significant cash and shares in a newly spun-off company focused on environmental and rail businesses. The deal, expected to close mid-2026, aims to unlock value and enhance operational flexibility.

data.title: FTSE MIB Drops to Four-Week Low
data.description:
The FTSE MIB dropped 0.8% to around 42,500 in early trading on Friday, reaching a four-week low and heading for its worst week since March, as investor anxiety around AI and tech stocks returned.

Adding to cautious sentiment was fading Federal Reserve rate cut expectations, while investors tracked developments over reports of a US–Russia peace plan for Ukraine that would reportedly require Kyiv to cede territory.

Among individual stocks, tech-oriented Prysmian and defence stock Leonardo led losses, shedding 3.6% and 4%, respectively.

Eni also fell more than 1% amid dropping oil prices.

Other notable laggards included Tenaris (-1.2%), Banco BPM SPA (-1%), and Unipol Gruppo (-1.1%).

On the flipside, Stellantis (+1.3%), Infrastrutture Wireless (+1.2%), and Davide Campari-Milano (+1.3%) were in the green.

data.title: Euro Area Manufacturing Sector Weakens
data.description:
The HCOB Flash Eurozone Manufacturing PMI slipped to 49.7 in November 2025, a five-month low, down from 50 in October and below expectations of 50.2.

Both new orders and employment declined, with manufacturing employment now falling on a monthly basis for two and a half years.

Output rose only marginally.

Meanwhile, input costs increased for the first time in three months and at the sharpest rate in eight months, while selling prices were left unchanged.

Business sentiment improved, with optimism reaching a five-month high.

However, Dr. Cyrus de la Rubia, Chief Economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank, noted that “companies have reduced their inventories of both intermediate goods and finished goods even more sharply than in the previous month, meaning that the inventory cycle continues to show no signs of turning upward.

We are still several months, and possibly even several quarters, away from sustained expansion in the manufacturing sector”.
